subject: Science
chapter: The World of Metals and Non-metals

activities_projects.title: Amphoteric Nature Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Prove that certain metal oxides react with both acids and bases
steps: Prepare aluminium oxide by heating aluminium foil
Divide the oxide into two test tubes
Add dilute HCl to the first and NaOH to the second
Observe the dissolution in both and write the complex chemical equations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the concept of amphoterism in specific metals like Aluminium and Zinc
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct chemical equations and explanation of dual reactivity (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reactivity Series Quantitative Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Predict and verify displacement outcomes using the reactivity series
materials: Solutions of Zinc Sulphate, Iron Sulphate, Copper Sulphate
Strip of Zn, Fe, and Cu
steps: Perform a matrix of 9 reactions (each metal in each solution)
Record where reactions occur and where they do not
Construct a self-derived reactivity series based on the number of displacements observed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Derives the relative positions of metals in the activity series through empirical data
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical construction of the series and accuracy of observations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Electrolytic Refining Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the industrial purification of metals
steps: Set up the electrolytic cell with the impure copper at the positive terminal
Pass current for 20 minutes
Observe the deposition on the cathode and the 'anode mud' at the bottom
Calculate the mass transfer if a digital scale is available
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the process of electrometallurgy and purification
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed diagram and explanation of ion movement (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The Chemistry of Aqua Regia
activities_projects.objective: Research the dissolution of noble metals (Theoretical)
steps: Investigate the ratio of concentrated HCl and $HNO_3$ in Aqua Regia
Explain why individual acids cannot dissolve gold but their mixture can
Write the chemical reaction involving nitrosyl chloride ($NOCl$)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores advanced oxidation and complexation of unreactive metals
activities_projects.evaluation: Technical accuracy of the chemical mechanism explained (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Non-Metal Allotropy Study
activities_projects.objective: Compare the physical properties of carbon allotropes
steps: Measure the electrical resistance of graphite vs. charcoal
Test the hardness of both by trying to scratch a glass surface
Explain how the atomic arrangement leads to such drastic differences in properties
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how structure dictates the properties of non-metallic allotropes
activities_projects.evaluation: Scientific report on structural-property relationships (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Sacrificial Anode Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate cathodic protection to prevent corrosion
steps: Wrap one iron nail tightly with magnesium ribbon
Leave both the wrapped and unwrapped nail in salt water
Observe which nail rusts and explain the protective role of the more reactive metal
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies the reactivity series to real-world marine and pipeline engineering
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct explanation of the 'sacrificial' mechanism (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Thermal Decomposition of Metal Carbonates
activities_projects.objective: Compare the stability of different metal carbonates
steps: Heat both carbonates separately and pass the gas into lime water
Time how long it takes for the lime water to turn milky for each
Determine which metal carbonate is more thermally stable
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the reactivity of a metal to the stability of its compounds
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical conclusion on thermal stability trends (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Synthesis of a Non-Metallic Acid
activities_projects.objective: Observe the formation of Sulphurous acid
steps: Burn sulphur and collect $SO_2$ gas in the jar
Add a small amount of water and shake to form $H_2SO_3$
Test the resulting liquid and write the two-step chemical equation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the synthesis of oxoacids from non-metal oxides
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in identifying the product and balanced equations (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Metal Ion Flame Test
activities_projects.objective: Identify metals based on their characteristic emission spectra
materials: Salts of Copper, Strontium, Potassium, and Sodium
steps: Clean the wire with HCl and dip it into a salt sample
Place it in the blue flame of the burner
Record the specific color produced (e.g., Apple green for Barium, Violet for Potassium)
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Introduces the concept of electronic transitions and analytical chemistry
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct identification of unknown salts based on flame color (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Case Study: The Iron Pillar of Delhi
steps: Research the composition of the Iron Pillar (specifically high phosphorus content)
Explain the formation of the 'Misawite' protective layer
Write a report on why this 1600-year-old structure has not rusted
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Appreciates historical metallurgy and advanced corrosion resistance
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of technical research and presentation quality (10 marks)
